key_insight: Theoria verifies AI answers by rewriting them into auditable, typed state transitions so hidden assumptions become explicit and every change is justified.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical middle ground between rigid formal proof systems and opaque LLM judges for high-stakes AI verification workflows.
method: Rewrite candidate solutions into sequences of typed proof-state transitions.
Require each transition to be licensed by an explicit justification such as a citation, computation, or given fact.
Audit completeness of change so any unlicensed mutation is flagged as a hidden premise.
evidence_signal: On HLE-Verified Gold, Theoria certified 105/185 problems with 91.4% strict precision, and on 95 adversarial poisoned proofs it caught 94.7% versus 83.2% for holistic judging.
watch_item: Performance gains are strongest for hidden-premise and fabricated-citation errors, so deployment still needs robust state rewriting and careful handling of error types where no formal advantage is predicted.
paper_title: Theoria: Rewrite-Acceptability Verification over Informal Reasoning States
